_evidence_check.cited_evidence: The observed change to 319 K in 10 seconds fixes the water-temperature decline rate for that case at a retained fraction of \(319/353\) after 10 seconds.

parsed_verdict.evidence: The observed change to 319 K in 10 seconds fixes the water-temperature decline rate for that case at a retained fraction of \(319/353\) after 10 seconds.
parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rules do not state a single universal per-second rate across all systems. Rule 5 treats the water-temperature decline as a separately-fixed rate ('fixes the water-temperature decline rate for that case'), failing N4 (universality) and N6 (no ~0.99/s rate stated for temperature; 319/353 over 10 s ≈ 0.989/s is close numerically but the rules do not assert the rate is the same as the motion rate).
